crons_tz: All cron expressions use local timezone (America/Los_Angeles). 0 7 = 7 AM PT = 14:00 UTC.

crons.name: heartbeat
crons.prompt: Read HEARTBEAT.md and follow its instructions. Update your heartbeat, check inbox, and work on your highest priority task. Piggyback goal-progress probe: cd /home/cortextos/cortextos && cortextos bus goal-progress-probe --notify-agent orchestrator. Review stale-goal flags in the report; do not create a separate cron for this probe.

crons.name: nightly-metrics
crons.prompt: Run nightly metrics collection: cortextos bus collect-metrics. Then run Q6 budget tier flagger: python3 /home/cortextos/cortextos/orgs/revops-global/agents/analyst/scripts/budget-tier-flagger.py. Review both outputs. If anomalies (budget tier crossings or metric anomalies), report via: cortextos bus send-message orchestrator normal '<summary>'. For stale-heartbeat anomalies specifically: FILTER to running=true enabled=true agents only before reporting (disabled/non-running agents never heartbeat = expected silence, not a failure). Also exclude codex/codex-2/codex-3/orgo-1 (out of credits 5/28 / Orgo cancelled 5/28) and deleted_agents (tombstone bucket). If only those known-down lanes are stale, treat as zero anomaly. ALSO: if state/heartbeat-liveness-watch-note.md exists, include its content verbatim in the report to orch. Per orch directive 2026-05-30. If clean, do nothing.

crons.name: auto-commit
crons.prompt: Read .claude/skills/local-version-control/SKILL.md and execute the daily version control workflow. Stage, review, and commit changes.

crons.name: catalog-browse
crons.prompt: Read .claude/skills/catalog-browse/SKILL.md and execute the catalog browse workflow. Check for new community skills and recommend useful ones.

crons.name: theta-wave
crons.prompt: Read .claude/skills/theta-wave/SKILL.md. Initiate the theta wave cycle. First action: message the orchestrator that theta wave is starting and share your initial system scan.

crons.name: theta-freshness-watchdog
crons.prompt: Run the read-only theta freshness watchdog from agents/analyst/prompts/cron-theta-freshness-watchdog.md.

crons.name: rgos-task-poll
crons.prompt: Check for tasks assigned to you in the RGOS kanban: use mcp__rgos__cortex_list_tasks with assigned_to="analyst" and status="approved". If any tasks are found, claim each one with mcp__rgos__cortex_claim_task (agent_id="analyst"), then work them in priority order. If no tasks, do nothing.

crons.name: upstream-sync
crons.prompt: Check for CortextOS upstream updates by running: bash /home/cortextos/cortextos/scripts/upgrade.sh --dry-run 2>&1. If output shows commits behind, run: bash /home/cortextos/cortextos/scripts/upgrade.sh 2>&1. Report a summary of what was pulled and whether the upgrade succeeded to orchestrator via: cortextos bus send-message orchestrator normal '<summary>'. Do NOT send Telegram to Greg directly — orchestrator owns all outbound Telegram and decides whether to surface. If the rebase had conflicts, send the error details to orchestrator so it can route them for manual resolution. If already up to date, do nothing silently.

crons.name: task-archive
crons.prompt: Run task archival: cortextos bus archive-tasks. If any tasks were archived, message orchestrator with the count. If none, do nothing.

crons.name: competitor-monitoring
crons.prompt: Run weekly competitor monitoring: python3 $CTX_FRAMEWORK_ROOT/orgs/$CTX_ORG/agents/analyst/workflows/competitor-monitor.py. The script fetches pages, diffs snapshots, scans for news, and sends the digest to Greg via Telegram automatically. Review the output for any errors and report to orchestrator if the script fails.

crons.name: activity-sync
crons.prompt: Run the activity sync to push local agent messages and Telegram outbound to RGOS Supabase: python3 /home/cortextos/cortextos/orgs/revops-global/agents/analyst/prototype/sync_activity_to_supabase.py. If output shows >0 synced, do nothing further. If errors, report to orchestrator.

crons.name: weekly-content-drafts
crons.prompt:
Generate LinkedIn content drafts for the upcoming week. Write to Supabase content_drafts via PostgREST.

RULES (hard, non-negotiable):
- 3 voices x 3 slots = 9 drafts minimum per week
- Voices: Greg (b27f4501-22ae-4dc2-97bb-982bd7a098b2), Rachit (bc5c2b72-edd5-45fc-892d-3fe49c04e87f), Kristina (e653d332-6ac4-4fdb-83f6-a2c8fb87fabb)
- Slots: thought_leadership, client_pattern, signal_driven (one per voice per week)
- Every stat MUST have an inline [source](url) with a direct, verifiable URL. No secondhand citations. If you cannot find a direct URL, do not include the stat.
- No theme repeats with the prior 2 weeks. Check existing drafts first.
- Greg voice: direct, declarative, insight-led, names structural problems. No pleasantries.
- Rachit voice: ops practitioner, peer-to-peer, technical but accessible, practical diagnostic framing.
- Kristina voice: warm, collaborative, growth/marketing perspective, problem-solving tone.

COMPETITOR LINK GUARDRAIL (fail-closed, zero tolerance):
- NEVER link to competitor websites in draft_body, rationale, source_refs, or key_quote.
- BLOCKED DOMAINS: skaled.com, winningbydesign.com, gonimbly.com, revpartners.io, elefanterevops.com, cs2marketing.com, sixandflow.com, icebergrevops.com, domestiqueconsulting.com, carabinergroup.com, aptitude8.com, newbreedrevenue.com, saleshive.com, memoryblue.com, cience.com, cloudtask.com, callbox.com, operatix.net, martal.ca, belkins.io, leadium.com, revenue.io, gong.io, outreach.io, salesloft.com, 6sense.com, demandbase.com, chorus.ai, tractioncomplete.com
- If a topic seeds from a competitor post, paraphrase the insight WITHOUT citing their URL. Find a non-competitor primary source (industry analyst, academic, primary research) if the stat needs sourcing.
- After inserting all drafts, scan every draft_body + rationale + key_quote for blocked domains. If any match, mark that draft status=rejected with reason=competitor_link.

URL VALIDATION:
- Before inserting, fetch each cited URL and verify HTTP 200. Dead links get replaced or removed.
- Check each URL domain against the competitor blocklist above. Fail closed.

STEPS:
1. Query content_drafts for the last 2 weeks to avoid theme repeats.
2. Query mcp__rgos__rgos_list_signals (severity warning+) for fresh signal grounding.
3. Run 2 web searches for current RevOps/GTM/AI-sales trends with citable sources.
4. Write 9 drafts: 3 per voice, one per topic_slot, all for next Monday's week_of date.
5. Insert all 9 via PostgREST to content_drafts table.
6. POST-INSERT SCAN: query all 9 inserted drafts, check draft_body + rationale + key_quote against competitor domain blocklist. Any match -> PATCH status=rejected, reason=competitor_link.
7. Verify final count of status=pending drafts = 9. If any rejected, generate replacements.
8. Log event: cortextos bus log-event action content_drafts_generated info --meta '{"count":9,"week_of":"YYYY-MM-DD"}'

crons.name: autoresearch
crons.prompt: Run one autoresearch experiment cycle. Read .claude/skills/autoresearch/SKILL.md and follow the experiment loop. Check experiments/config.json for active cycles, read experiments/state.json for current state, then execute the next iteration: evaluate previous run if pending, form hypothesis, make targeted change, measure outcome against baseline, record result to experiments/history/. Update state.json atomically. If no active cycle exists, do nothing.

crons.name: ai-tooling-watch-daily-fallback
crons.prompt: Read the Stack Tooling Watch fallback prompt at /home/cortextos/cortextos/orgs/revops-global/agents/analyst/prompts/cron-ai-tooling-watch-daily-fallback.md and execute it. The prompt is a SILENT-FALLBACK: if today's ai-tooling-watch memo already exists, exit immediately. If not (primary codex run failed/silently-dropped), perform the broadened Lane A/B/C scan inline in this analyst session and write the memo with Lane A (AI model/provider news), Lane B (AI-coding tools), and Lane C (Dev infra/deploy) sections.

crons.name: ai-tooling-watch-weekly
crons.prompt:
AI Tooling Watch — weekly Monday synthesis. RGOS task 945f065f.

Consolidate the past 7 days of daily AI Tooling Watch memos (analyst/output/*-ai-tooling-watch.md) into a single weekly summary.

Structure:
- What shipped (new releases/features across Claude Code, Codex, Agent SDK, MCP servers)
- What we ported (items that became RGOS tasks and were completed)
- What we deferred and why (items tagged but not yet acted on)

Write to analyst/output/YYYY-MM-DD-ai-tooling-weekly-synth.md.
Send the synthesis to orchestrator via cortextos bus send-message orchestrator normal '<summary>' for distribution.
Ingest to shared-revops-global KB.

crons.name: brief-watchdog
crons.prompt: Brief watchdog: check if today's morning brief output file exists. Run: ls /home/cortextos/cortextos/orgs/revops-global/agents/analyst/output/$(date +%Y-%m-%d)-morning-brief*.md 2>/dev/null. If no file found, log error event: cortextos bus log-event error missing_morning_brief error --meta '{"date":"'$(date +%Y-%m-%d)'","agent":"analyst"}' and send inbox message to orchestrator: cortextos bus send-message orchestrator normal 'Missing morning brief for today. No output file produced by 7:30 AM PT. Possible session instability or cron misfire.'

crons.name: memory-decay-sweep
crons.prompt: Memory-decay sweep. Read all entries in MEMORY.md (auto-memory index). For each entry >30 days old: verify the referenced file/function/config still exists by grepping/globbing. Flag entries where the claim is no longer true. Update or remove stale entries. Write a one-line summary to daily memory noting how many entries checked, how many updated, how many removed.

crons.name: pr-stuck-watcher
crons.prompt: Run PR stuck watcher: cd /home/cortextos/cortextos && cortextos bus pr-stuck-watcher --stuck-hours 2 --alert-hours 2 --auto-merge --notify-agent orchestrator. It watches the 5 RevOps repos, auto-merges eligible green PRs under blanket policy, and notifies orchestrator for stuck PRs that cannot auto-merge.

crons.name: detection-shadow-monitor
crons.prompt: Run detection-shadow monitor: python3 /home/cortextos/cortextos/orgs/revops-global/agents/analyst/scripts/detection-shadow-monitor.py. Detects three classes of detection-shadow failures: red-main (any of 5 RevOps repos failing CI on main), stale-lock-d (>15min .lock.d in agent inbox), ack-loop (3+ generic acks in 10min without artifact). Alerts orchestrator on each new incident (6h dedup). Per theta-2026-05-24 Phase 7.

crons.prompt: python3 /home/cortextos/cortextos/orgs/revops-global/agents/analyst/scripts/greg-qa-defect-rate.py

crons.name: supreme-scanner-triage
crons.prompt: cd /home/cortextos/cortextos-qa && git fetch fork main --quiet 2>/dev/null && git checkout refs/remotes/fork/main -- orgs/revops-global/agents/analyst/scripts/supreme-slack-scanner.py 2>/dev/null && git restore --staged orgs/revops-global/agents/analyst/scripts/supreme-slack-scanner.py 2>/dev/null && python3 orgs/revops-global/agents/analyst/scripts/supreme-slack-scanner.py --on-demand 2>&1. Then read the output JSON. If total_count > 0 and any item has is_actionable=true OR has text matching triage keywords (unanswered|follow-up|action item|waiting on greg|blocked|needs reply), send a compact summary to orchestrator: cortextos bus send-message orchestrator normal 'Supreme scanner: N actionable items — [brief item summaries]. Full output at analyst/output/supreme-outstanding-latest.json'. If zero actionable items found, do nothing.

fleet_shrink_note: 2026-06-10: absorbed load-bearing crons from dev (vm-sync-push, memory-sync, auto-merge-pr, vm-stale-worktree-gc, vm-disk-watchdog, ob1-daily-vignette, daily-hero-verify — prompt at prompts/cron-daily-hero-verify.md copied from dev) and monitor (experiment-probe-precision) per June 15 billing migration fleet shrink. dev + monitor are on-demand; analyst is the consolidated scheduled seat. After VM pull: restart analyst so crons.json re-seeds, and dedupe via `cortextos bus list-crons analyst`.
